questions.questionText: The most common benign tumor of the uterus is:
questions.optionB: Leiomyoma (fibroid) — estrogen-dependent, occurs in 20-40% of women of reproductive age
questions.explanation: Leiomyoma/fibroid: most common benign tumor of uterus (and most common pelvic tumor in women). Estrogen-dependent — grows during reproductive years, shrinks after menopause. Types by location: intramural (most common), subserosal (may pedunculate), submucosal (most symptoms — menorrhagia, infertility). Features: menorrhagia (most common symptom), dysmenorrhea, pressure symptoms (urinary frequency, constipation), infertility. Red/carneous degeneration: during pregnancy (pain). Treatment: medical (GnRH agonists, ulipristal), myomectomy (fertility preservation), hysterectomy (definitive). Reference: DC Dutta — Fibroid.

questions.questionText: Dermoid cyst (mature cystic teratoma) of the ovary:
questions.optionB: Is the most common benign ovarian tumor in young women — contains ectodermal derivatives (hair, teeth, sebum)
questions.explanation: Mature cystic teratoma (dermoid): most common benign ovarian tumor in reproductive age (15-45 years). Most common ovarian germ cell tumor overall. Contains tissues from all 3 germ layers: ectoderm (skin, hair, teeth — most prominent), mesoderm (bone, cartilage), endoderm (thyroid — struma ovarii). USG: 'dermoid mesh/plug', calcification, fat-fluid level. Bilateral in 10-15%. Torsion: most common complication. Malignant transformation: 1-2% (usually squamous cell carcinoma). Rokitansky protuberance: solid projection. Reference: DC Dutta — Dermoid Cyst.
